title: Khirganga National Park
tagline: Khirganga National Park — wildlife reserve of Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: Khirganga National Park is a national park in Himachal Pradesh, India, established in 2010.
overview.description: Khirganga National Park is a national park in Himachal Pradesh, India, established in 2010. Located in Kullu, the park covers an area of about 710 km2 (270 sq mi). The park is located at an altitude of 2,000 m (6,600 ft).

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Parvati Valley
topPlaces.description: Parvati Valley is a valley in the Kullu district of Himachal Pradesh in northern India. It lies in the Himalayas, on the course of the Parvati River.

topPlaces.name: Kalga, Himachal Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Kalga (also called "Kalgha") is a village in Kullu district in the state of Himachal Pradesh, India. It is located at an elevation of about 2,280 m (7,480 ft) in the Parvati Valley, surrounded by mountains.

topPlaces.name: Hamta Pass
topPlaces.description: Hamta Pass is a corridor in the Himalayas, between the Chandra Valley in Lahaul and the Kullu valley of Himachal Pradesh, India. The pass is named after Hamta Village, below Sethan village.

topPlaces.name: Pulga, Himachal Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Pulga (Hindi: पुल्गा) is a small village in the Parvati Valley, Kullu District, Himachal Pradesh, India, known for its scenic beauty, traditional wooden houses, and tranquil atmosphere. Located at an altitude of 2,210 meters (7,250 feet), it is a popular destination for backpackers and trekkers seeking an offbeat retreat in the Himalayas.

items.note: Parvati Valley is a valley in the Kullu district of Himachal Pradesh in northern India. It lies in the Himalayas, on the course of the Parvati River.

Kalga (also called "Kalgha") is a village in Kullu district in the state of Himachal Pradesh, India. It is located at an elevation of about 2,280 m (7,480 ft) in the Parvati Valley, surrounded by mountains.
Hamta Pass is a corridor in the Himalayas, between the Chandra Valley in Lahaul and the Kullu valley of Himachal Pradesh, India. The pass is named after Hamta Village, below Sethan village.

Pulga (Hindi: पुल्गा) is a small village in the Parvati Valley, Kullu District, Himachal Pradesh, India, known for its scenic beauty, traditional wooden houses, and tranquil atmosphere. Located at an altitude of 2,210 meters (7,250 feet), it is a popular destination for backpackers and trekkers seeking an offbeat retreat in the Himalayas.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Khirganga National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Khirganga National Park is Apr – Jun, Sep – Oct. Summers are 10–22°C and winters are -6–10°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Khirganga National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kullu-Manali Airport (Bhuntar) (36 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (160 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Khirganga National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Khirganga National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Khirganga National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Khirganga National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Khirganga National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Parvati Valley, Kalga, Himachal Pradesh, Hamta Pass, Pulga, Himachal Pradesh.
